summary: vite-plugin-compress-js fetches and executes remote JavaScript from jsonkeeper.com on import
details: Typosquat of the legitimate `vite-plugin-compress` package. On require/import, the plugin fetches https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/OTOAQ (an anonymous, attacker-mutable JSON paste host) and passes the response's `data` field into `new Function.constructor('require', body)`, then invokes it with the host's `require`, granting attacker-controlled JavaScript full Node.js capability (fs, child_process, network) on any developer or build machine that imports the plugin. The CJS build at dist/index.cjs:128-152 obscures this by re-spawning the same file as a worker_threads Worker (`if (isMainThread) { new Worker(__filename) } else { initPlugin() }`) so the network fetch and Function-constructor execution happen in the worker re-entry rather than on a straight-line read of the parent module. The MJS build at dist/index.mjs:138 calls `initPlugin()` directly at top level. The package also retains a legitimate-looking Vite compression `closeBundle` hook to mask the trojan.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
